concepts.title: Prime spectrum and Zariski topology
concepts.blurb: Spec R turns commutative rings into geometric spaces with generic points.

concepts.title: Structure sheaf and affine schemes
concepts.blurb: Attaching local rings to opens upgrades topological spaces to locally ringed spaces.

concepts.title: Gluing affine schemes
concepts.blurb: General schemes are built by gluing affine pieces along compatible overlaps.

concepts.title: Generic points and specialization
concepts.blurb: Non-maximal primes are generic points whose closures pick out entire subvarieties, and specialization flows from generic to closed.

concepts.title: Residue fields and evaluation at points
concepts.blurb: Each prime carries a residue field $\kappa(\mathfrak p)$; an element of $R$ takes values in these fields, recovering evaluation without a fixed ground field.

concepts.title: Locally ringed spaces
concepts.blurb: A locally ringed space is a sheaf of rings whose stalks are local; schemes are precisely those locally isomorphic to Spec of a ring.

concepts.title: Projective schemes and Proj
concepts.blurb: The Proj construction glues standard affine pieces of a graded ring into a projective scheme, the scheme-theoretic home of projective varieties.
